action.reasoning: Observation: direct TripoSR baseline is valid and gives LPIPS 0.6191, but metrics are extremely poor, indicating the built-in render camera sequence does not match dataset cameras. Analysis: the main bottleneck is camera mismatch, not training. Since TripoSR API docs only expose render(n_views) and not custom camera poses, a practical improvement is to learn a view-synthesis model that maps the input image plus target camera delta to the target view directly. With only 800 train objects and 11 targets each, a lightweight conditional UNet can be trained quickly and should beat the mismatched baseline. Decision: implement a camera-conditioned image-to-image model, train for a few epochs to get a fast second baseline, test local prediction, and keep predict.py compatible with both learned model and fallback TripoSR.
